name: Katha Upanishad
summary: The Katha Upanishad presents the dialogue between young Nachiketa and Yama, the god of death. Nachiketa asks for the secret of death and immortality. Yama reveals the nature of the Atman — smaller than the smallest, greater than the greatest — and the path to liberation through discrimination and self-control.

verses.sanskrit:
उशन् ह वै वाजश्रवसः सर्ववेदसं ददौ |
तस्य ह नचिकेता नाम पुत्र आस ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration:
uśan ha vai vājaśravasaḥ sarvavedasaṃ dadau |
tasya ha naciketā nāma putra āsa ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: Vajashravasa, desiring the fruit of the sacrifice, gave away all his possessions. He had a son named Nachiketa.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: वाजश्रवसः — वाजश्रवसः पुत्रः — सर्ववेदसं सर्वस्वं ददौ — विश्वजिद्यागे।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Vajashravasa, son of Vajashravas, gave away all his wealth in the Vishvajit sacrifice, desiring its fruits. His son Nachiketa observed this.

verses.sanskrit:
अणोरणीयान् महतो महीयानात्मास्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम् |
तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः ||१.२.२०||
verses.transliteration:
aṇor aṇīyān mahato mahīyān ātmāsya jantor nihito guhāyām |
tam akratuḥ paśyati vītaśoko dhātuprasādān mahimānam ātmanaḥ ||1.2.20||

word_meanings.meaning: hidden in the cave (of the heart)
by the grace of the Creator

verses.english_translation: The Self, smaller than the smallest, greater than the greatest, is hidden in the heart of this creature. One who is free from desire and grief, by the grace of the Creator, sees the glory of the Self.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अणोः अणीयान् — परमाणोरपि सूक्ष्मतरः। महतः महीयान् — महत्तत्त्वादपि महत्तरः। गुहायाम् — हृदयाकाशे — निहितः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self is subtler than the subtlest atom and vaster than the vastest. It is hidden in the cave of the heart. Only one who is free from desire and sorrow, by the grace of the Lord, can perceive the glory of the Self.

verses.english_translation: Verse 21 of the Katha Upanishad. Full content will be added.
Verse 22 of the Katha Upanishad. Full content will be added.

verses.sanskrit:
नायमात्मा प्रवचनेन लभ्यो न मेधया न बहुना श्रुतेन |
यमेवैष वृणुते तेन लभ्यस्तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूं स्वाम् ||१.२.२३||
verses.transliteration:
nāyam ātmā pravacanena labhyo na medhayā na bahunā śrutena |
yam evaisa vṛṇute tena labhyas tasyaiṣa ātmā vivṛṇute tanūṃ svām ||1.2.23||

word_meanings.meaning: by that one it is attained

verses.english_translation: This Self cannot be attained by instruction, nor by intellect, nor by much learning. It is attained by the one whom it chooses — to that one the Self reveals its own form.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: न प्रवचनेन — बहुशास्त्राध्ययनेन न लभ्यः। न मेधया — तर्कशक्त्या न गृह्यते। यम् एव एषः वृणुते — यस्मिन् प्रसीदति — तस्मै स्वयं प्रकाशते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self cannot be known through mere study of scriptures, nor through intellectual reasoning, nor through vast learning. It reveals itself only to the one it chooses — the one in whom it is pleased. This grace comes through sincere devotion and purity of heart.
